quickFacts: Cheapest excellent beer in Europe — €1-2 per pint
Charles Bridge at sunrise ≫ Charles Bridge at mid-day
Czech crown, not Euro — ATMs give the best exchange rate
Avoid Wenceslas Square restaurants — tourist trap central
foodScene: Svíčková (beef sirloin in cream sauce with dumplings) is the national treasure. Trdelník (chimney cake) is a tourist invention — but tasty. Pilsner Urquell at the source. Lokál Dlouhá for authentic Czech pub food. Craft beer at BeerGeek Bar.

places.name: Prague Castle & St. Vitus
places.caption: World's largest castle complex
places.description: The castle grounds are free to enter. St. Vitus Cathedral's stained glass (including Mucha's window) is breathtaking. Go at opening to avoid crowds.

places.name: Charles Bridge
places.caption: Gothic bridge at dawn
places.description: 30 Baroque statues, buskers, and the best views of the castle. Visit at 6am for magical emptiness, or at night when it's floodlit.

places.name: Vinohrady & Žižkov
places.caption: Local neighbourhood life
places.description: Skip Old Town Square restaurants. Vinohrady has the best cafés, beer gardens, and the Žižkov TV Tower with David Černý's crawling babies.

places.name: Old Town Square
places.caption: Astronomical Clock central
places.description: The Astronomical Clock show is underwhelming but the square itself is gorgeous. Climb the Old Town Hall tower for aerial views.

places.name: Letná Park
places.caption: Panoramic beer garden
places.description: The best sunset viewpoint in Prague, with a beer garden serving Pilsner with city panoramas. The giant metronome is a local icon.

places.name: Vyšehrad Fortress
places.caption: Ancient hilltop fortress
places.description: Quiet, uncrowded, with river views. The cemetery holds Dvořák and Mucha. The casemates are atmospheric. Most tourists never make it here.

places.name: John Lennon Wall
places.caption: Ever-changing street art
places.description: This graffiti wall has been repainted countless times since the 1980s. It's a symbol of free speech and a colourful photo spot.

places.name: Petřín Hill
places.caption: Mini Eiffel Tower & gardens
places.description: Take the funicular up, wander rose gardens, climb the lookout tower, and get lost in the mirror maze. A peaceful escape from Old Town.

places.name: Náplavka Riverbank
places.caption: Farmers market & river bars
places.description: Saturday farmers market with local food and crafts. On warm evenings, the floating bars on the Vltava are the place to be.

places.name: Day Trip: Kutná Hora
places.caption: Bone church & silver mines
places.description: The Sedlec Ossuary is decorated with 40,000 human bones. Morbidly fascinating and only an hour by train from Prague.

places.name: Prague Castle (Pražský hrad)
places.caption: World's largest castle complex
places.description: 70,000 sqm complex with St Vitus Cathedral, Old Royal Palace and Golden Lane. Views over the city from the ramparts.

places.name: Old Town Square & Astronomical Clock
places.caption: Medieval clockwork
places.description: 600-year-old astronomical clock performs on the hour. Týn Church spires, Jan Hus memorial and pastel Baroque houses.

places.name: Malá Strana
places.caption: Lesser Town
places.description: Baroque quarter below the castle with hidden gardens, the John Lennon Wall, St Nicholas Church and cosy wine bars.

places.name: Jewish Quarter (Josefov)
places.caption: Historic ghetto
places.description: Six synagogues, the Old Jewish Cemetery with 12,000 tombstones layered over centuries, and the powerful Pinkas Memorial.

places.name: Prague Beer Culture
places.caption: World's best beer country
places.description: Czechs drink more beer per capita than anywhere. Pilsner Urquell invented lager. Try U Fleků (brewing since 1499) and craft bars.

places.name: Lennon Wall
places.caption: Peace graffiti
places.description: Wall near the French Embassy covered in Beatles lyrics and peace messages since the 1980s. A living artwork that changes daily.

places.name: Dancing House
places.caption: Deconstructivist landmark
places.description: Gehry & Milunić's 1996 'Fred and Ginger' building. Rooftop bar and restaurant with Vltava views. Architecture photo favourite.
